rulings.Case Title: REBECCA B. MANCE VS. STATE FARM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 16, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF REBECCA MANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication fails to identify each cause of action, claim, issue of duty or affirmative defense. The separate statement of undisputed material facts does not separately set them out, and the notice of motion does not reference them. (See: California Rules of Court 3.1350 et seq. and Code of Civil Procedure 437c). Plaintiff fails to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff does not discuss each element for causes of action against Levin, and fails to show how the evidence presented satisfies each element. C.C.P. 437c makes no provision for adjudication of liability alone where issues of the calculation of damages remain. Dept. of Industrial Relations v. U I Video Stores, Inc. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1084, 1097. The court need not address defendant's objections to evidence. =(302/CWW)
